dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we're about to make a terrible mistake, and I'm the only one who sees it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A scout returned to {location} claiming they found a {enemy_type} staging camp {location_hint}. The militia is arming up to attack at dawn. But I spoke to {target_npc}, who traveled that road last week, and they described a group of refugees sheltering in the same area: families fleeing violence, not causing it. The scout saw what they expected to see, not what was actually there. I need you to reach the camp before the militia does and bring back {quest_item} as proof that these are people, not enemies.
dialogueChunks.outro: If the militia attacks innocent refugees, we become the monsters. We have until dawn to prove them wrong.

playerResponses.text: Innocents in the crossfire. I'll get there first.
Racing a militia to save refugees. No pressure. What's the reward?
Getting between a militia and its target is a good way to get killed.

dialogueChunks.intro: They're blaming {target_npc} for the sickness, {player_name}. And they're wrong. Dead wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three children in {location} fell ill after drinking water from the eastern well. Someone started a rumor that {target_npc} poisoned it, because {target_npc} was seen adding herbs to the well last week. What nobody bothered to ask is that {target_npc} was treating the water with purifying agents because they noticed contamination upstream. The real source of the poison is somewhere {location_hint}, and I'll bet the {enemy_type} that moved into the area are responsible. I need {quest_item} from the contamination site to prove where the sickness actually came from.
dialogueChunks.outro: A healer who tried to help is about to be punished for someone else's crime. That's not justice: that's ignorance.

playerResponses.text: I'll trace the contamination to its source. Healers shouldn't be punished for healing.
Investigating a poisoning near enemy territory. Risky. What's the compensation?
Witch hunts are dangerous to interfere with. I'll pass.

dialogueChunks.intro: Everyone in {location} loves the new trader, {player_name}. Everyone except me.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A merchant arrived in {location} a month ago offering {quest_item} at prices that make no sense. Too cheap, too generous, too eager to be everyone's friend. {target_npc} is suspicious too but was shouted down when they raised concerns. I've noticed the merchant making trips {location_hint} at night, and the {enemy_type} attacks have shifted to target everywhere except routes the merchant uses. That's not coincidence. I need you to follow the merchant's trail and find out what they're really doing out there.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement is so grateful for cheap goods that they can't see they're being set up. Someone has to look past the smile.

playerResponses.text: If this merchant is working with the enemy, the settlement needs to know. I'll investigate.
Following a suspected enemy agent through hostile territory. Name your price.
Maybe they're just a generous merchant. Not every kindness hides a knife.
